title: [Feature]: Add fan-out partial-failure recovery logging and metrics
problem: When one message is routed to multiple sinks, operators need to understand which route matched, which child sinks were selected, which required sinks committed, which optional sinks timed out or failed, and whether the original JetStream message was ACKed. Without dedicated metrics and sanitized logs, partial fan-out failures are difficult to troubleshoot and can be mistaken for either full success or full loss.
proposal: Add fan-out-specific counters, gauges, and structured log events. Candidate metrics include routed messages, no-route messages, selected child sink count, required child success, required child failure, optional child success, optional child timeout, optional child failure, ACK-gate wait duration, and fan-out batch duration. The metrics model should integrate with nats-sink-metrics and the observability core while keeping subject and sink labels disabled by default unless an explicit policy permits them.
users: Operators, reliability engineers, security reviewers, and mission-support teams who need evidence about multi-destination event custody and optional copy behavior.
delivery_semantics: Metrics must observe fan-out behavior without changing ACK, retry, DLQ, or sink write decisions. A metrics exporter failure must not cause ACK to happen early, suppress a sink failure, or create unbounded memory growth. Logs must clearly distinguish required sink failures from optional sink failures.
security: Metrics and logs must be sanitized. Payloads, secrets, connection strings, exact sensitive subjects, file paths, mission labels, classification values, and child sink details must not be exported unless policy explicitly allows them. High-cardinality labels must be off by default.
acceptance: Fan-out metrics cover route match, selected child sinks, required success and failure, optional success and failure, optional timeout, and ACK-gate wait time.
nats-sink-metrics can display fan-out counters without requiring Prometheus.
Sanitized logs explain partial failure in human-readable terms without payloads or secrets.
Unit tests cover metric updates for successful fan-out, required failure, optional timeout, and no-route behavior.
Documentation includes metric names, meanings, example CLI output, and privacy controls.
tests: Add metrics unit tests and CLI output tests. Add smoke tests with mock sinks that simulate required success, required failure, optional success, and optional timeout. Verify subject-aware and sink-name labels remain disabled unless policy enables them.
documentation: Update docs/metrics.md, docs/observability.md, docs/observability/prometheus.md, docs/sink-framework.md, docs/operations.md, docs/security.md, and CHANGELOG.md when implemented.
closeout: Close only after a release containing fan-out observability is published. Close-out evidence must include metric names, sample output, tests run, documentation updates, and privacy limitations.
